tie_break_note: Tie-break limitation: live tables are ordered by points, then goal difference, then goals for, then team name (alphabetical) as a deterministic fallback. The full FIFA/FIF8A tie-breakers (head-to-head record, head-to-head goal difference/goals, disciplinary/fair-play points, drawing of lots) are NOT applied here. Final standings may differ when teams are level on points and goal difference.
sim_tie_break_note: Ranking within a group uses points -> goal difference -> goals for -> uniform random draw. The 8 best third-placed teams are chosen by the same ladder. Head-to-head and fair-play tie-breakers are not modelled.

semantics.submitted_predictions: Frozen candidate files are read-only after submission; live results do not rewrite or regenerate submitted group score predictions.
semantics.played_group_matches: Played group matches use actual scores from data/live/scores_override.csv for live tables and prediction-vs-actual scoring.
semantics.unplayed_group_matches: Unplayed group matches keep using the frozen active candidate's scoreline distribution for simulations.
semantics.group_advancement: Advancement probabilities combine actual played results with simulated unplayed results.
semantics.knockout_state: After the group stage is complete, actual group standings and the best third-placed teams feed official Annexe C and the Round-of-32 bracket mapping. Later played knockout matches must pin actual winners and only future matches may be simulated.

actual_bracket_state.note: Actual bracket assignment is pending until all group-stage matches have final scores.

knockout_predictions.note: Knockout match predictions refresh round by round: projected teams are the up-front gamble; once actual participants are known the current recommendation uses them. Submitted group-stage predictions are never modified.

human_upside_overlay.label: Context only — not used in final prediction.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Güler/Yıldız generation, clear attacking roles, strong cohesion.
teams.analyst_note: One of strongest non-favourite upside duos; reporting suggests squad cohesion.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Yamal/Cubarsí Barcelona/La Masia continuity is elite chemistry evidence.
teams.analyst_note: Yamal Ballon/Kopa signal; elite variance.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Vitinha/João Neves PSG midfield connection is elite chemistry evidence.
teams.analyst_note: Vitinha Ballon #3 plus João Neves Kopa-tier.

teams.chemistry_rationale: If Dembélé/Doué are selected, PSG chemistry is strong; if Mbappé/Doué, more national-team based.
teams.analyst_note: Elite attack; multiple PSG/Real Madrid star options.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Elite quality but limited same-club chemistry and Tuchel tactical fit needs validation.
teams.analyst_note: Huge talent but tactical/chemistry debate prevents bigger boost.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Haaland/Nusa fit stylistically but not elite same-club chemistry.
teams.analyst_note: Haaland is pure scoreline-upside.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Elite individuals, but attacking hierarchy/roles need validation; no automatic chemistry boost.
teams.analyst_note: Huge attacking variance; validate Neymar/primary star role and fitness.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Potter reset positive; star/talent link not club-based.
teams.key_absence_rationale: Important player but long-known absence; Sweden have built the squad around Gyokeres/Isak/Bergvall without him. Do not double-count.
teams.analyst_note: Gyökeres form and Potter effect create positive overlay.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Settled pragmatic structure and Salah release pattern create strong functional chemistry.
teams.analyst_note: Salah carry factor; squad motivation appears high.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Stable structure plus elite young attacking upside.
teams.analyst_note: Strong defensive structure plus elite young upside.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Strong 2022 continuity and coherent transition identity; Hakimi-El Khannouss is not same-club chemistry but team structure is strong.
teams.analyst_note: Ben Seghir is absent; El Khannouss is the best validated young creative replacement in the squad.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Díaz-Arias-James style attacking structure gives strong functional chemistry even without Durán.
teams.analyst_note: Jhon Durán is absent; Jhon Arias is not a pure youth talent but is the best high-impact selected co-star alongside Díaz.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Messi-era continuity and long-standing winning core.
teams.analyst_note: Messi can still decide games; age/minutes risk.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Moriyasu continuity, harmony and collective automatisms are strong.
teams.analyst_note: Stable disciplined technical side.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Strong national-team continuity relative to fame level.
teams.analyst_note: Fayzullaev gives genuine underdog breakout upside.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Mature, tactically clear core; Xhaka as organiser.
teams.analyst_note: Xhaka tempo-setter; stable mature squad.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Transition profile; chemistry boost modest.
teams.analyst_note: Wide/transition upside and positive warmup signal.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Stable experienced core; no elite star/talent automatisms.
teams.analyst_note: Experienced side with playoff momentum; Schick scoreline upside.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Strong team identity but unclear direct star/talent automatisms.
teams.analyst_note: Physical intense side; Enciso gives upside.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Strong national identity and midfield intensity; chemistry is structural rather than a direct star-talent club link.
teams.analyst_note: Luciano Rodríguez is absent; Pellistri is the best selected wide/upside replacement in a Bielsa-style system.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Bazeley continuity through age groups and Wood centrality boost cohesion.
teams.analyst_note: Wood carry factor; Eli Just season improves talent signal.

teams.chemistry_rationale: System side with clear roles and strong collective structure.
teams.analyst_note: System-driven upside, stable profile.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Exceptional unity/no-stress identity; star/talent club chemistry less direct.
teams.analyst_note: No-stress morale and diaspora support; Logan Costa injury risk.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Wirtz connects with technical core, but chemistry edge is moderate.
teams.analyst_note: Wirtz difference-maker; broader team quality already in model.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Home-country and Liga MX familiarity, but star/talent pair needs validation.
teams.analyst_note: Home pressure plus striker upside; validate final star/talent choice against squad.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Doku/Fernandez-Pardo is high-variance but not established partnership; Garcia has improved atmosphere.
teams.analyst_note: Doku variance; ageing core adds uncertainty.

teams.chemistry_rationale: National-team familiarity, not same-club chemistry.
teams.analyst_note: Son carry-factor, but preparation/form questions.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Technical core and growing belief; chemistry boost modest.
teams.analyst_note: Technical upside and positive warmup signal.

teams.chemistry_rationale: No strong star/talent connection; team structure still developing.
teams.analyst_note: Team form positive but Davies/Bombito fitness risk matters.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Youth upside, but no proven attacking automatisms yet.
teams.analyst_note: Bold youth selections create variance.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Historic run, Dutch-Caribbean identity, strong collective belief.
teams.analyst_note: Historic unbeaten run and strong morale.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Local/Sundowns-heavy stability helps, but star/talent connection is not elite.
teams.analyst_note: Keeper can steal points; attacking ceiling modest.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Van Dijk leadership remains, but injuries and the loss of Simons lower attacking chemistry and role clarity.
teams.key_absence_rationale: Simons removes creative upside, but do not overstate if local recent-lineup evidence shows he was not a locked starter. If local data confirms he had not been starting regularly for Oranje, cap residual penalty at -0.25.
teams.analyst_note: Xavi Simons absence removes the main creative breakout profile; Hato is the best young validated replacement but less attacking.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Mané leadership but talent link is unproven.
teams.analyst_note: Mané leadership; Ndiaye breakout story, but age risk.

teams.chemistry_rationale: National-team continuity, but talent role needs validation.
teams.key_absence_rationale: Late warm-up injury to a player involved in Scotland's qualifying core; less likely to be captured by existing model inputs.
teams.analyst_note: Goal threat plus youth energy; validate talent choice.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Stable CONCACAF core and Carrasquilla hub.
teams.analyst_note: Carrasquilla hub; limited individual ceiling.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Hannibal is central and Lamouchi believes in Gharbi; chemistry is modest.
teams.analyst_note: Excellent qualifying defence but limited star ceiling.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Stable regional core around Al-Taamari.
teams.analyst_note: Clear star but limited supporting cast.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Familiarity exists, but role/fitness/environment uncertainty weakens chemistry.
teams.analyst_note: Pulisic reliable; US mood/pressure and injury noise reduce confidence.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Kudus/Djiku absences reduce cohesion; Semenyo-Nuamah is more attacking variance than proven automatisms.
teams.key_absence_rationale: Kudus is a genuine star/carry-player absence, but the injury occurred in January and Ghana have already played warm-ups without him. This is a creative-ceiling loss, not a late-shock absence. Secondary Salisu/Djiku defensive injuries are audited as non-stacking review context and kept in current-state/chemistry rather than an additional residual penalty.
teams.analyst_note: Kudus absence lowers the ceiling; Semenyo becomes the best attacking carry option, with Nuamah as transition/upside talent.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Physical transition threat, but limited proven automatisms between star and talent.
teams.analyst_note: Silas is absent; Mukau is the best selected young breakout replacement but likely more midfield upside than scoreline carry.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Domestic familiarity exists, but manager/preparation uncertainty reduces cohesion.
teams.analyst_note: Big-stage player but uneven preparation and manager change.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Domestic-league familiarity helps Afif, but broader preparation/coaching context weakens total.
teams.analyst_note: Afif is real; preparation and Lopetegui results are a concern.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Džeko leadership exists, but chemistry upside is limited by age/tempo profile.
teams.analyst_note: Džeko quality but age/tempo risk.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Mahrez dependence; no positive chemistry adjustment.
teams.analyst_note: Older-star dependence; validate form and role.

teams.chemistry_rationale: No strong star/talent automatisms; resilience more than chemistry.
teams.analyst_note: Resilience narrative strong, but external disruption and limited ceiling.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Excellent core continuity, but age risk caps total.
teams.analyst_note: Modrić aura remains, but age risk dominates.

teams.chemistry_rationale: Experienced core exists, but environment/context remains negative and cohesion confidence is low.
teams.analyst_note: Allahyar is absent; Yousefi is a validated squad replacement but offers limited breakout signal.

objective_residual.no_manual_label: No manual sign-off or subjective override used.
